stages.name: Election Announcement
stages.description: The governing authority officially declares that an election will be held. Key dates are published including nomination deadlines, voting day, and when results will be declared.
key_actions: Election dates announced to the public
stages.citizen_action: Stay informed — follow official announcements for key dates in your area.

stages.name: Voter Registration
stages.description: Citizens who wish to vote must register with the election authority. This establishes the official voter roll used on election day.
key_actions: Voter rolls are verified and updated
Voter ID cards or confirmation issued
stages.citizen_action: Check if you are registered. If not, register before the deadline using official government portals.

stages.name: Candidate Nomination
stages.description: Individuals or political parties formally file their intention to contest the election. Nominations are reviewed for eligibility.
key_actions: Nomination forms submitted with required documents
stages.citizen_action: Review the list of candidates in your constituency once published.

stages.name: Election Campaign
stages.description: Candidates and parties present their policies and platforms to voters through rallies, media, and outreach. Campaign spending is regulated.
key_actions: Candidates hold public rallies and debates
stages.citizen_action: Attend debates, read manifesto documents, and critically evaluate candidate promises.

stages.name: Voting Day
stages.description: Registered voters visit polling stations to cast their ballots. The process is overseen by election officials and independent observers to ensure fairness.
key_actions: Polling stations open at designated time
Voters present ID and cast secret ballots
Polls close and ballot boxes sealed
stages.citizen_action: Bring valid voter ID, go to your assigned polling station, and cast your vote. Your vote is secret.

stages.name: Vote Counting
stages.description: Sealed ballot boxes are opened and votes tallied under strict supervision. Representatives of candidates are present to observe the count.
key_actions: Ballot boxes transported to counting centers
stages.citizen_action: Follow live result updates through official election commission channels.

stages.name: Result Declaration
stages.description: The election authority officially declares the winners after verifying all counts. Results are published in the official gazette.
key_actions: Losers may file legal challenges if evidence exists
stages.citizen_action: Review official results. Understand your right to petition if you believe there were irregularities.

stages.name: Transition of Power
stages.description: The winning candidate or party is sworn in and assumes their official duties. The outgoing authority hands over responsibilities in an orderly manner.
key_actions: New government or official begins duties
stages.citizen_action: Engage with your newly elected representative. Democracy continues between elections.
